q: Sound is described as a sequence of what type of waves?
Through what type of media does sound propagate?
During propagation, sound waves can undergo which of the following processes?
Which property of media determines the speed of sound within the medium, affected by temperature?
a: A relationship between density and pressure

q: The rate at which sound is attenuated is determined by which property of the medium?
a: The specific gravity of the medium
The dielectric constant of the medium
The magnetic permeability of the medium

q: For many media, such as air or water, attenuation due to viscosity is considered what?
Besides density and temperature, what other property affects the speed of a sound wave?
How does the speed of sound vary across different media?
a: It will be different for different media
It will be the same for all media
It increases with the density of the media
It decreases with the elasticity of the media

q: In the formula for the speed of sound in a solid medium, $v = \sqrt{\frac{Y}{\rho}}$, what does $Y$ represent?
In the formula for the speed of sound in a solid medium, $v = \sqrt{\frac{Y}{\rho}}$, what does $\rho$ represent?
In the formula for the velocity of sound in liquids, $v = \sqrt{\frac{B}{\rho}}$, what does $B$ represent?
In the formula for the velocity of sound in liquids, $v = \sqrt{\frac{B}{\rho}}$, what does $\rho$ represent?
In the formula for the velocity of sound in gases, $v = \sqrt{\frac{\gamma p}{\rho}}$, what does $\gamma$ represent?
a: the specific heat ratio of gas

q: In the formula for the velocity of sound in gases, $v = \sqrt{\frac{\gamma p}{\rho}}$, what does $p$ represent?
In the formula for the velocity of sound in gases, $v = \sqrt{\frac{\gamma p}{\rho}}$, what does $\rho$ represent?
How do longitudinal sound waves travel in solids compared to liquids and gases?
a: faster in solids than in liquids than in gases
slower in solids than in liquids than in gases
at the same speed in all three media
faster in liquids than in solids

q: Young's modulus and the bulk modulus are described as what type of properties of the medium?
What are the SI units of Young's modulus?
What are the SI units of the bulk modulus?
The speed of sound within a medium is determined by a relationship between density and pressure, which is also affected by what factor?
Sound is a sequence of pressure waves which propagates through media such as air or what other substance?
When sound waves propagate, they can be attenuated by the medium. What does attenuation mean in this context?
Which of the following is considered an elasticity property of the medium?
What type of sound waves travel faster in solids than they do in liquids and gases?
